rationale: The page contains a small section of content about Sundarbans Tiger Reserve in West Bengal, but the majority of the page is dominated by a large advertisement for 'Vision IAS Smart Mapping Classes'. Therefore, the page is classified as an advertisement. Only the relevant content about the tiger reserve has been extracted.
cleanedText:
West Bengal
Sundarbans Tiger Reserve (STR)
Context: STR is now India's second largest after expansion of its area was approved by National Board of Wildlife (NBWL).
Now STR is just behind Andhra Pradesh's Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve in size.
Location: Coastal districts of West Bengal.
Only mangrove forest (with Bangladesh) housing a significant tiger population.
Borders: Bangladesh (east), Bay of Bengal (south), River Matla (west), Rivers Bidya & Gomdi (north-west).
